text.title: anyhow for Application Code
text.summary: When and how to use anyhow at binary boundaries versus typed errors in library crates.

rule.statement: Use anyhow::Result at binary/main.rs boundaries. Use thiserror enums in library crates and at public API surfaces. Never add anyhow::Error as a thiserror variant in a library — it collapses the type system.
rule.rationale: anyhow::Error is type-erased. A library returning anyhow::Error forces callers to downcast to match on failure kind. thiserror preserves the variant for matching.

rule.statement: Set RUST_BACKTRACE=1 in production and ensure anyhow::Error::backtrace() is surfaced in structured logs. Without this, anyhow errors lose stack context in release builds.
rule.rationale: Release builds strip debug info. RUST_BACKTRACE=1 enables the libstd capture mechanism that anyhow uses. Without it, error chains show only the message chain.
